textByHashes.230fba932877c1195e4e1b2ffeab6287fa9fa524: Not much to figure out there, the problem is those thieving bandits.
textByHashes.2bf6c6d9513f66dc34a7976d2fdb6c9f045f7529: They are always stealing, starting fights and disrupting the lives of the townsfolk.
textByHashes.4c2459e39f4eae7c0ed38bd6f404bc4b194f2d7c: Well, we also steal, start fights, and disrupt lives - but we do it with class! And the bandits definitely started it.
textByHashes.8bbeb63377c6275eb80af891cda2c96c3ca40a74: I can scarcely remember, it's been so long.
textByHashes.bff3b9af380e4f308ad9a9069c990e18d6445901: I think our leader once mentioned that they robbed one of our camels. A heinous crime around these parts.
textByHashes.599ce655abc6f759d51d2321bbf651f19f09a6e9: Those bandits would never agree to that. They couldn't afford even the mangiest excuse for a camel, never mind the desert traversing, four legged beauty that we would demand.
textByHashes.9a08b413b8ef4ab63665475edddec59d2b4954b0: A mere receipt? For a discount camel? I knew those bandits were cheap! Tell them that we have changed our minds and demand 10 camels in return for our mercy.
textByHashes.046df344f26f36e488f4f450ad206a57264d2f8d: Greetings Adventurer, Ali wants to have a word with you.
textByHashes.88514512f99f60c72db3409da4717ad6beb43a07: Ali, our Chief of Operations. He should be somewhere nearby the tent.
textByHashes.1e7558e889ec4869d972fe9a86482fcb69646484: Tumeken be praised, you have seen the error of your ways. May Elidinis the river goddess bless your lands.
textByHashes.b049f136d0caddf35ea8a5d71035ea402c510f97: You're better off asking Ali the Operator. He's the brains behind the whole operation.
textByHashes.626afa6f792d2e4fa06060dcbe96d4ee56a75d64: Tell me what your problem is and I'll see what I can do.
textByHashes.1e21115589c0769e569a6557e3783ca23f0dda36: I just can't believe one of my friends could be a traitor.
textByHashes.1b1deeaf6205ecbb1cfd14bc67c58cd1a216152c: Well, I suppose there is Traitorous Ali.
textByHashes.843f518562398787798174ef9262a6126cdeddf6: Oh yes. The most untrustworthy, backstabbing little rat to scamper across Tumeken's fair desert. He'd sell his own mother to slavers if he hadn't already betrayed her and had her locked up for the last job he pulled.
textByHashes.5cdc3cecaa6a9fba788611a4a35dd34c7f5d3a6b: Positive, he was turned by the offer of a set of waterproofs and a ride on a camel.
textByHashes.5fb63672a9c404a619280d620b0e2032d936735c: Well what can I say? We don't call him 'Ali of the Good Decisions', do we?
textByHashes.b4ae670402669261e09e47e971e1c1988e1763e5: You treacherous jackal! Prepare to die!
textByHashes.516f7315775164637c0b8e7be4fb30db8b156d85: You treacherous jackal! I should never have trusted you.
textByHashes.1720c46d98a0fc7607517840eeea86308582f1a0: I'm too busy for chitchat. Come back if you have some business to discuss with us.
